🏃 Health and Physical Education

Personal Hygiene

Learn how to keep yourself clean, healthy, and happy every day!

📚 Unit 1 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 1: Personal Hygiene

Personal hygiene means keeping your body clean and healthy. Good hygiene helps you look good, feel good, and stay free from diseases.

Lesson 1: Care of Sense Organs

We have five sense organs. They help us see, hear, smell, taste, and touch the world around us. We must take good care of them.

The Five Sense Organs

Sense OrganFunctionBody Part
EyesSightSee things
EarsHearingListen to sounds
NoseSmellDetect odors
TongueTasteRecognize flavors
SkinTouchFeel texture, temperature, pain

Care of Eyes

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Wash eyes with clean water dailyRub eyes with dirty hands
Read in proper lightRead in dim or very bright light
Keep a proper distance from books/TVSit too close to the screen
Eat vitamin A-rich foods (carrots, papaya)Look directly at the sun
Wear sunglasses in strong sunlightUse someone else’s spectacles
Visit an eye doctor regularlyWatch screen for too long

💡 Good to know: Carrots are rich in Vitamin A, which is good for eyesight.

Care of Ears

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Clean ears with a soft clothInsert sharp objects into ears
Dry ears after a bathLet water stay in ears
Keep volume low on headphonesListen to very loud music
See a doctor for ear painIgnore ear infections

💡 Never use: Cotton swabs, pins, or matchsticks inside your ear. They can damage your eardrum!

Care of Nose

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Breathe through your noseBreathe through your mouth (when possible)
Blow your nose gently into a tissuePick your nose with fingers
Cover your nose in dusty areasInhale strong chemicals or smoke
Keep your surroundings cleanIgnore nosebleeds or breathing problems

Care of Tongue (Taste)

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Brush your tongue when brushing teethEat very hot food that can burn your tongue
Drink enough waterUse very spicy things excessively
See a doctor if you lose tasteIgnore mouth ulcers or sores

Care of Skin (Touch)

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Take a bath every dayGo to bed without bathing
Apply moisturizer if skin is dryScratch insect bites (can cause infection)
Wear clean clothesWear tight or rough clothes for long
Protect skin from extreme sun/coldIgnore rashes, cuts, or burns

Lesson 2: Importance of Clean Clothes

Wearing clean clothes is an important part of personal hygiene.

Why Should We Wear Clean Clothes?

ReasonExplanation
Prevent diseasesDirty clothes carry germs
Look neat and tidyClean clothes make a good impression
Feel fresh and comfortableSweat and dirt can cause itching
Protect the bodyClothes shield us from heat, cold, and insects
Boost confidenceYou feel better when you look clean

How to Keep Clothes Clean

MethodHow to do it
WashingUse soap or detergent and clean water
DryingDry clothes in sunlight (sunlight kills germs)
IroningIroning kills remaining germs and makes clothes neat
StorageKeep clothes in a clean, dry cupboard
ChangingChange undergarments daily; change other clothes when dirty

Types of Clothes for Different Weather

WeatherType of clothesExamples
Hot (Summer)Light-colored, cotton, looseCotton shirt, shorts, sandals
Cold (Winter)Dark-colored, woolen, thickSweater, jacket, socks, cap
RainyWaterproofRaincoat, umbrella, gumboots

💡 Why light colors in summer? Light colors reflect sunlight and keep you cool. Dark colors absorb heat.

Lesson 3: Exercise, Rest and Sleep

A healthy body needs a balance of exercise, rest, and sleep.

Exercise

Exercise means moving your body to keep it fit and strong.

Benefits of Exercise

BenefitExplanation
Strong muscles and bonesMakes your body stronger
Healthy heartImproves blood circulation
Good weightHelps control body weight
Fresh mindReduces stress and makes you happy
Better sleepYou sleep well after physical activity
Disease preventionReduces risk of many diseases

Types of Exercise

TypeExamples
Aerobic (with oxygen)Running, swimming, cycling, skipping
Strength trainingPush-ups, sit-ups, climbing
StretchingYoga, bending, touching toes
PlayFootball, cricket, kho-kho, kabaddi

💡 How much exercise? At least 30-60 minutes of physical activity every day.

Rest

Rest means giving your body a break from activity.

Why is Rest Important?

ReasonExplanation
Muscles recoverMuscles repair themselves during rest
Energy is restoredYour body refills its energy stores
Prevents tirednessAvoids burnout and exhaustion
Better performanceRested bodies work better

Types of Rest

TypeWhat it means
Short breaksRest for 5-10 minutes between activities
RelaxationSitting quietly, listening to music, deep breathing
SleepThe most important form of rest

Sleep

Sleep is when your body and mind completely rest and recharge.

How Much Sleep Do Children Need?

AgeHours of sleep needed
5-6 years old10-12 hours
7-9 years old10-11 hours
10-12 years old9-10 hours

💡 Class 5 students (10-11 years) need about 9-10 hours of sleep every night!

Good Sleep Habits (Sleep Hygiene)

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Go to bed at the same time every nightWatch mobile/TV in bed
Sleep in a dark, quiet roomDrink tea/coffee before bed
Take a warm bath before bedEat a heavy meal just before sleeping
Read a book to relaxUse mobile phones in bed
Keep your bed clean and comfortableStudy or play on your bed

What Happens If You Don’t Get Enough Sleep?

ProblemEffect
Feeling tiredCannot focus in class
IrritabilityGet angry or upset easily
Poor memoryForget what you learned
Weak immunityFall sick more often
Slow growthGrowth hormones are released during sleep

Lesson 4: Menstrual Hygiene

Menstruation (periods) is a normal biological process that happens in girls and women. It usually starts between ages 9 and 15.

What is Menstruation?

Key PointExplanation
What is it?Monthly shedding of the uterine lining through the vagina
How long?Usually 3-7 days
How often?Every 21-35 days
Who has it?Females from puberty to menopause (about ages 9-50)

Why is Menstrual Hygiene Important?

ReasonExplanation
Prevent infectionsThe body is more sensitive during periods
Stay comfortableGood hygiene prevents rashes and odor
Avoid embarrassmentFreshness and cleanliness help confidence
Protect long-term healthPoor hygiene can lead to reproductive problems

Menstrual Hygiene Tips

TipHow to do it
Use clean pads/clothChange sanitary pads every 4-6 hours
Wash properlyWash the genital area 2-3 times a day
Dispose safelyWrap used pads in paper and throw in a dustbin (not toilet)
Wear clean underwearChange underwear daily
Bathe regularlyTake a bath every day during periods
Change clothes if wetWear clean, dry clothes

Common Myths and Facts

Myth ❌Fact ✅
Menstruating girls should not batheBathing keeps you clean and healthy
Menstruation is a diseaseIt is a normal, healthy body process
Period blood is dirtyIt is just blood and tissue
Girls cannot exercise during periodsLight exercise is good and reduces pain
Menstruating girls cannot cook or touch plantsNo scientific reason – this is a superstition

What to Eat and Avoid During Periods

Eat ✅Avoid ❌
Warm water and soupsCold drinks and ice cream
Fruits and vegetablesToo much salt
Iron-rich foods (spinach, dates)Oily and fried food
Whole grains (oats, brown rice)Too much sugar
Ginger and turmeric teaCaffeine (coffee, cola)

How to Help a Friend or Sister During Periods

Way to help
Be understanding and kind
Do not make fun or tease
Offer a hot water bag for cramps
Help get a pad if needed
Do not treat it as a secret or shameful topic

💡 Remember: Menstruation is normal and natural. Every girl experiences it. There is nothing to be ashamed of!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. We have ______ sense organs.
  2. Carrots are rich in ______, which is good for eyes.
  3. We should sleep for at least ______ hours every night.
  4. ______ means keeping your body clean and healthy.
  5. During menstruation, sanitary pads should be changed every ______ hours.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. We should look directly at the sun to keep eyes healthy. (T/F)
  2. Cotton clothes are good for summer. (T/F)
  3. Sleep is not important for children. (T/F)
  4. Menstruation is a disease. (T/F)
  5. We should bathe daily during periods. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Sense Organ to Its Function

Sense OrganFunction
Eyesa) Hearing
Earsb) Taste
Nosec) Sight
Tongued) Smell
Skine) Touch

Exercise 4: Answer in One Sentence

  1. Why should we wear clean clothes?
  2. What are two benefits of exercise?
  3. Why is rest important after physical activity?
  4. What is menstruation?

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. five, 2. Vitamin A, 3. 9-10, 4. Personal hygiene, 5. 4-6

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Eyes→c, Ears→a, Nose→d, Tongue→b, Skin→e

Great work! Good personal hygiene keeps you healthy, confident, and ready to learn. Take care of yourself every day!
